title: Litigation Archive Management System Review: CaseArchive vs LitigationFile 2026 Comparison
description: Compare CaseArchive and LitigationFile litigation archive management systems. Analyze case archives, evidence archives, judgment archives, and archive retr.
content:
Litigation Archive Management System Overview

Litigation archive management systems handle litigation case archives, evidence, and judgments. In 2026, CaseArchive and LitigationFile are two leading professional products. This review compares their archive management capabilities.

Case Archives

Archive FeaturesCaseArchiveLitigationFile
Case ClassificationSmart case classificationManual case classification
Case AssociationCase association analysisBasic case association
Case TimelineVisual timelineBasic time sorting
Case TrackingCase progress trackingNo case tracking

Evidence Archives

CaseArchive offers powerful evidence archive features, supporting evidence classification, evidence association, and evidence chain management. LitigationFile has basic evidence archive features. For evidence archives, CaseArchive is recommended.

Judgment Archives

LitigationFile provides comprehensive judgment archive features, supporting judgment storage, judgment analysis, and judgment citation. CaseArchive has basic judgment archive features. For judgment archives, LitigationFile is recommended.

Archive Retrieval

LitigationFile has powerful archive retrieval with full-text search, keyword search, and search reports. CaseArchive has basic retrieval features. For archive retrieval, LitigationFile is recommended.

Archive Security

CaseArchive offers comprehensive archive security with access control, encrypted storage, and audit logs. LitigationFile has basic security features. For archive security, CaseArchive is recommended.

Price and Selection

CaseArchive annual fee: 150,000 CNY; LitigationFile annual fee: 100,000 CNY. For evidence archives and security, choose CaseArchive. For judgment archives and retrieval, choose LitigationFile.
author: Litigation Archive Expert

mainEntity.name: What features should I look for in legal document management software?

acceptedAnswer.text: Key features include document automation, version control, search capabilities with OCR, security compliance (SOC 2, HIPAA), workflow management, e-signature integration, and mobile access for remote work.

mainEntity.name: How much does legal document management software typically cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: Pricing ranges from $20-50/user/month for basic plans to $100-300/user/month for enterprise solutions. Implementation costs vary based on customization needs and training requirements.

mainEntity.name: What security standards should legal document software meet?

acceptedAnswer.text: Look for SOC 2 Type II compliance, encryption at rest and in transit, audit trails, role-based access control, and integration with firm-wide security policies.

mainEntity.name: Can legal document management integrate with existing law firm systems?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most platforms offer API integrations with practice management software, CRM systems, billing platforms, and Microsoft Office/Google Workspace.

mainEntity.name: What are the main benefits of document automation for law firms?

acceptedAnswer.text: Benefits include reduced drafting time (40-60%), improved accuracy, consistent formatting, faster client turnaround, and reduced compliance risks.

howto_schema.name: How to Choose Litigation Archive Management System Review: CaseArchive vs LitigationFile 2026

step.name: Assess Requirements
step.text: Evaluate firm size, document volume, compliance needs, and budget to define requirements.

step.name: Research Platforms
step.text: Compare vendors based on features, security, integration capabilities, and user reviews.

step.name: Request Demos
step.text: Schedule demonstrations with 2-3 top vendors to see real workflows and ask questions.

step.name: Check Compliance
step.text: Verify SOC 2, HIPAA, and other relevant certifications; review audit reports.

step.name: Plan Implementation
step.text: Define timeline, data migration strategy, training plan, and success metrics.
